name: The Bank Of New Glarus
description_short: Independent community bank headquartered in New Glarus, Wisconsin, serving southern Wisconsin since 1893 with personal banking, business lending, and wealth management.
description_long:
The Bank of New Glarus is an independent community bank that opened on October 30, 1893, making it the first official bank in New Glarus, Wisconsin. Founded with $12,000 in opening capital as a five-way partnership, it was incorporated under state charter in 1903 and has grown to become the largest deposit bank in Green and Lafayette Counties. The bank maintains its headquarters in New Glarus and operates branch offices in seven additional communities: Belleville, Brodhead, Darlington, Gratiot, Juda, Monroe, and New Glarus—a total of 8 community locations throughout southern Wisconsin.

The Bank of New Glarus offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services including personal banking (checking, savings, CDs), mortgage loans, home equity loans, business banking, commercial lending, agricultural lending, and wealth management services. Their current promotional offerings include 6-month CDs at 3.75% APY and a mobile banking app for convenient account access. They employ dedicated residential and commercial lending teams, with specialized lenders for mortgages, business real estate, lines of credit, and agricultural financing.

What distinguishes The Bank of New Glarus is its 132-year commitment to local community banking and its role as a market leader in deposit banking within its region. The bank emphasizes personalized service through branch teams of lending experts and maintains strong local roots, having invested in its communities throughout its history. Their Swiss-style headquarters building, constructed in 1969, and the addition of drive-up facilities demonstrate their commitment to modernizing customer convenience while maintaining community presence.

As a regional community bank, The Bank of New Glarus is best suited for customers in southern Wisconsin seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making and personalized service. However, potential customers should note that their service area is geographically limited to southern Wisconsin, and they may lack the technology infrastructure or product breadth of larger national banks. The bank's specific rates, loan terms, and eligibility requirements are not detailed on their website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Real estate loans for business properties
Online account opening for select products
pros: 132-year history as first bank in New Glarus, demonstrating stability and deep community roots
Largest deposit bank in Green and Lafayette Counties with 8 branch locations across southern Wisconsin
Dedicated lending teams with specialized residential and commercial lenders available to consult
Offers full-service banking including personal accounts, CDs, mortgages, home equity loans, and business lending
Mobile banking app for convenient account access and management
Competitive CD rates (6-month CDs at 3.75% APY advertised)
Services include agricultural lending, distinguishing them from purely consumer-focused banks
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to southern Wisconsin only, not available for non-regional customers
Specific interest rates, loan terms, fees, and APRs not disclosed on website beyond single CD promotion
No online account opening information available for most products; primarily promotes in-branch relationship banking
Wealth management services mentioned but no details provided on offerings, investment options, or advisory services
No information about deposit insurance, FDIC membership, or other regulatory protections clearly stated on public pages
best_for: Small business owners and agricultural customers in southern Wisconsin seeking personalized lending relationships
Homebuyers in Green and Lafayette Counties looking for local mortgage lenders with community focus
Residents of the 8-community service area (New Glarus, Belleville, Brodhead, Darlington, Gratiot, Juda, Monroe) seeking full-service banking
diagnosis: The Bank of New Glarus is best for small business owners, farmers, and homebuyers in southern Wisconsin who value personalized, relationship-based banking from a locally-owned institution with over 130 years of regional presence. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves eight communities in southern Wisconsin, making it inaccessible for customers outside that region; additionally, detailed pricing and product terms are not publicly available on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Independent community bank in New Glarus, Wisconsin since 1893, offering personal and business banking.
